summaryReconciliation: Runner emitted 34/2/11; recomputed from testCases[] ledger (authoritative): 39 PASS, 1 FAIL (IMP-046), 7 BLOCKED (IMP-026, 027, 028, 030, 038, 040, 044), 0 SKIPPED.

testCases.title: Token Exchange Failure — impersonate_failed Error Handled
testCases.section: LoginOnBehalf > Error Paths > Token Exchange Failure
testCases.notes: Cannot simulate server-side token exchange failure via route interception in Playwright MCP context: setTimeout is not available in route handlers, and fetch-delay approach is not viable. Network error simulation requires server-side tooling not available in this context.

testCases.title: Silent Flow Error Path — Auth Error on Impersonation URL
testCases.notes: Cannot simulate silent flow auth error via route interception in Playwright MCP. Same constraint as IMP-026.

testCases.title: Loading State Disables Form Controls During Verification
testCases.notes: Sub-100ms disabled state cannot be observed in MCP context. setTimeout not available in Playwright route handlers. Transient loading state not capturable without native CDP instrumentation.

testCases.title: Place Order on Behalf — Full Checkout Flow Under Impersonation
testCases.notes: Full checkout flow requires pre-configured payment method and shipping. Complex multi-step setup not feasible in single-agent context. Core BL-AUTH-006/007 cart/order isolation already validated by IMP-020 and IMP-021.

testCases.title: RBAC Propagation — Removing loginOnBehalf Permission Stops Impersonation Mid-Session
testCases.notes: Requires two simultaneous browser contexts: one as operator (to be mid-impersonation) and one as admin (to revoke permission). Not achievable in single-browser-slot test runner without agent coordination.

testCases.title: Company Members — 'Login on behalf' Hidden When Target Has No Security Account
testCases.section: LoginOnBehalf > Storefront Entry Point > No Security Account
testCases.notes: Precondition not satisfiable: no contact-only member (member with no linked security account) present in TechFlow org member list. All current members have platform user accounts. Test requires seeding a contact-only member via Admin SPA.

testCases.title: Mobile Menu — 'Revert back to own account' Item Visible During Impersonation
testCases.section: LoginOnBehalf > Exit > Revert UI Presence > Mobile
testCases.notes: PASS. 'Zurück zu John Mitchell' (revert) visible in mobile menu. NOTE: touch target height is 24px (button h=24px), below the 44px minimum recommended. Advisory observation only — not a hard assertion in this test case.

testCases.title: VcLoaderOverlay with Slot Content Displayed During Impersonate and Revert Transitions
testCases.notes: VcLoaderOverlay transition is a sub-second transient state during full-page navigation. MutationObserver did not capture any overlay insertion/removal (empty observations array). The overlay is likely destroyed during navigation before it can be observed. Cannot verify slot content text without race-condition-free tooling.

testCases.title: Previous Impersonation Token Invalidated After Revert (AC#6 Security)
testCases.failedAssertion: POST-REVERT: GET /api/platform/security/currentuser with captured impersonation token returns HTTP 401 (AC#6: token invalidated)
testCases.notes: Pre-revert: GET /api/platform/security/currentuser with impersonation token = HTTP 200 (userName=test-david.kim-20260310@test-agent.com, id=ec3031ac). Revert completed (sub reverted to 143bc845/John Mitchell, no banner). Post-revert: same token returns HTTP 200 — NOT invalidated. AC#6 violated: impersonation access_token remains valid after operator reverts. Security regression per BL-AUTH-007 and BL-AUTH-011.

bugs.title: Impersonation Access Token Not Invalidated After Operator Reverts Session (VCST-4906 AC#6 Violation)
bugs.failedAssertion: GET /api/platform/security/currentuser with stale impersonation token returns HTTP 401 after revert
bugs.stepsToReproduce: 1. Sign in as John Mitchell (test-john.mitchell-20260310@test-agent.com) who has CanImpersonate permission. 2. Navigate to /account/impersonate/ec3031ac-6dd9-42e9-b7a7-0c10d9aac07b (David Kim). 3. Capture the impersonation access_token from localStorage['auth'].access_token. 4. Verify token is valid: GET https://vcst-qa.govirto.com/api/platform/security/currentuser with Bearer {token} → HTTP 200, userName=test-david.kim. 5. Open account menu → click 'Back to John Mitchell'. Wait for revert to complete (banner gone, sub=John Mitchell). 6. Re-send same GET /api/platform/security/currentuser with the CAPTURED impersonation token. Observe HTTP 200 instead of 401.
bugs.expected: POST-REVERT: GET /api/platform/security/currentuser with captured impersonation token returns HTTP 401. The impersonation access_token MUST be revoked server-side within the same request cycle as the revert operation (AC#6: 'Previous Token MUST be invalidated').
bugs.actual: POST-REVERT: GET /api/platform/security/currentuser with the captured impersonation token returns HTTP 200 (userName=test-david.kim-20260310@test-agent.com). The token remains valid indefinitely after the operator reverts their session.

confirmation.browser: playwright-firefox (cross-engine — different from preliminary chrome run)
confirmation.deltaNote: Time between revert click and post-revert assertion — well outside any animation/race window
confirmation.revocationSearchNote: Filter revoke|revocation|sign-out|signout|logout|introspect against full session network log returned zero matches. Only auth call during revert: POST /connect/token grant_type=impersonate&user_id= (empty user_id = revert) → 200, mints new operator token. Revert is pure client-side token swap.
scopeEscalations: xAPI GraphQL POST /graphql { me { ... } } with stale IMP_TOKEN → HTTP 200 returning David's profile (organizationId=6fb516c1 TechFlow). Rules out /currentuser caching artifact — two independent backend surfaces (Platform REST + xAPI GraphQL) accept the revoked token.

IMP_TOKEN still valid after the operator (John) fully signs out of the storefront. No backend revocation/sign-out path invalidates impersonation tokens at all — token lives its full 30-min exp regardless of revert or sign-out.
confirmation.rootCauseHypothesis: Frontend never calls a revoke endpoint and backend has no impersonation-session bookkeeping. /connect/token grant_type=impersonate mints a 30-min JWT that is server-trusted by signature + exp only; revert is a pure client-side token swap, leaving the impersonation JWT fully usable against Platform REST and xAPI GraphQL for its entire 30-min lifetime even after operator sign-out.
